revision.comment: syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext"
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} <!-- PLEASE ADD CATEGORIES AND INTERWIKIS AT THE BOTTOM OF THIS PAGE -->
This template is used to format the articles found in the '''''Selected publication''''' sub-page found on some Wikipedia Portal main pages. Use it on the article pages that are linked to the Selected publication sub-page, e.g. '''Portal:''Foo''/Selected publication/1'''. For the quickest and easiest way to create the '''Portal:''Foo''/Selected ''bar''''' sub-page and related article links, use one of the {{tl|Numbered subpages}} templates.
== Template layout == <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext" width="75%"> {{Selected publication | image = | size = | side = | caption = | title = | comic = | author = | artist = | publisher = | page = <!--- Required ---> | text = <!--- Required ---> | link = <!--- Required ---> }} </syntaxhighlight>
== Usage == It is unnecessary to include braces, quotation marks or other wiki-markup in the template. The '''''|text=''''' field does allow the use of wiki-markup at the contributor's discretion.
* '''image''' - Places an image relating to the subject of the article on the left side of the section. * '''size''' - Sets the image size, if left out or left blank it will default to 150px. Values must be entered '''''xxx''px'''. * '''side''' - this field sets the alignment of the image; acceptable values are ''right'' or ''center''. * '''caption''' - Creates a caption for the image. * '''title''' - the title of the work, it will automatically italicize it * '''comic''' - a "yes" in this field will add the option to include an artists name * '''author''' - the creator of the work * '''editor''' - a "yes" in this field will change the ''By'' to ''Edited by'' in the author field. It will not work if the comic switch is set to yes. * '''artist''' - The artist of the work, it will only work if the ''|comic='' field is set to yes * '''publisher'''- the publisher name is listed here * '''page''' - is used to set the page that the product list resides on, e.g. ''|name=comic'' would set the '''More selected ''publications''''' link to look for the sub-pages at '''Portal:Foo/Selected comic''' as well as setting the text of the link to '''More selected ''comics'''''. '''Do not pluralize!''' * '''text''' - A brief summary of the subject. * '''link''' - sets the link in the '''Read more...''' that points to the full article.
== Example ==
=== Generic === <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext"> {{Selected publication | image = File:Time Cover in 1924-08-25.JPG | side = left | caption = The cover shows the 1923 U.S. Women's Amateur Champion, Edith Cummings. | title = Time | publisher = [[Time Warner]] | text = '''''Time''''' (trademarked in capitals as '''''TIME''''') is an American [[news magazine]]... | link = Time (magazine) }} </syntaxhighlight>
will produce:
{{Selected publication | image = Time Cover in 1924-08-25.JPG | side = left | caption = The cover shows the 1923 U.S. Women's Amateur Champion, Edith Cummings. | title = Time | publisher = [[Time Warner]] | text = '''''Time''''' (trademarked in capitals as '''''TIME''''') is an American [[news magazine]], similar to ''[[Newsweek]]'' and ''[[U.S. News & World Report]].'' A European edition (''Time Europe'', formerly known as ''Time Atlantic'') is published from London. ''Time Europe'' covers the Middle East, Africa and, since 2003, Latin America. An Asian edition (''Time Asia'') is based in Hong Kong. As of 2009, ''Time'' no longer publishes a Canadian advertiser edition. The South Pacific edition, covering Australia, New Zealand and the Pacific Islands, is based in [[Sydney]]. In some advertising campaigns, the magazine has suggested that, through a [[Backronym|bacronym]], the letters ''T-I-M-E'' stand for '''''The International Magazine of Events'''''. | link = Time (magazine) }}
=== Comic ===
<syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext" width="75%"> {{Selected publication | image = | caption = The movie version of the Batmobile | title = [[Batman]] | comic = yes | creator = [[Bob Kane]], [[Bill Finger]] | author = [[Dennis O'Neil]] | artist = [[Frank Miller (comics)|Frank Miller]] | publisher = [[DC Comics]] | page = comics | text = '''Batman''' (originally referred to as '''the Bat-Man''' and still referred to at times as '''the Batman''') ... | link = Batman }} </syntaxhighlight>
will produce
{{Selected publication | image = | caption = The movie version of the Batmobile | title = [[Batman]] | comic = yes | creator = [[Bob Kane]], [[Bill Finger]] | author = [[Dennis O'Neil]] | artist = [[Frank Miller (comics)|Frank Miller]] | publisher = [[DC Comics]] | page = comics | text = '''Batman''' (originally referred to as '''the Bat-Man''' and still referred to at times as '''the Batman''') is a [[Character (arts)|fictional character]], a [[comic book]] [[superhero]] co-created by artist [[Bob Kane]] and writer [[Bill Finger]] (although only Kane receives official credit), appearing in publications by [[DC Comics]]. The character first appeared in ''[[Detective Comics]]'' #27 in May 1939. Batman's [[secret identity]] is '''Bruce Wayne''', a wealthy industrialist, playboy, and philanthropist. Witnessing the murder of his parents as a child, Wayne trains himself both physically and intellectually and dons a bat-themed costume in order to fight crime. Batman operates in the fictional American [[Gotham City]], assisted by various supporting characters including his sidekick [[Robin (character)|Robin]] and his butler [[Alfred Pennyworth]], and fights an assortment of villains influenced by the characters' roots in film and [[pulp magazine]]s. Unlike most superheroes, he does not possess any [[Superpower (ability)|superpowers]]; he makes use of intellect, detective skills, science and technology, wealth, physical prowess, and intimidation in his war on crime. | link = Batman }}
== See also == {{Portal template list}}
<includeonly>{{Sandbox other|| <!--Categories below here, please; interwikis to Wikidata.--> [[Category:Wikipedia Portal templates|Selected Publication]] }}</includeonly>

This template is used to format the articles found in the '''''Selected publication''''' sub-page found on some Wikipedia Portal main pages. Use it on the article pages that are linked to the Selected publication sub-page, e.g. '''Portal:''Foo''/Selected publication/1'''. For the quickest and easiest way to create the '''Portal:''Foo''/Selected ''bar''''' sub-page and related article links, use one of the {{tl|Numbered subpages}} templates.
== Template layout == <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext" width="75%"> {{Selected publication | image = | size = | side = | caption = | title = | comic = | author = | artist = | publisher = | page = <!--- Required ---> | text = <!--- Required ---> | link = <!--- Required ---> }} </syntaxhighlight>
== Usage == It is unnecessary to include braces, quotation marks or other wiki-markup in the template. The '''''|text=''''' field does allow the use of wiki-markup at the contributor's discretion.
* '''image''' - Places an image relating to the subject of the article on the left side of the section. * '''size''' - Sets the image size, if left out or left blank it will default to 150px. Values must be entered '''''xxx''px'''. * '''side''' - this field sets the alignment of the image; acceptable values are ''right'' or ''center''. * '''caption''' - Creates a caption for the image. * '''title''' - the title of the work, it will automatically italicize it * '''comic''' - a "yes" in this field will add the option to include an artists name * '''author''' - the creator of the work * '''editor''' - a "yes" in this field will change the ''By'' to ''Edited by'' in the author field. It will not work if the comic switch is set to yes. * '''artist''' - The artist of the work, it will only work if the ''|comic='' field is set to yes * '''publisher'''- the publisher name is listed here * '''page''' - is used to set the page that the product list resides on, e.g. ''|name=comic'' would set the '''More selected ''publications''''' link to look for the sub-pages at '''Portal:Foo/Selected comic''' as well as setting the text of the link to '''More selected ''comics'''''. '''Do not pluralize!''' * '''text''' - A brief summary of the subject. * '''link''' - sets the link in the '''Read more...''' that points to the full article.
== Example ==
=== Generic === <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext"> {{Selected publication | image = File:Time Cover in 1924-08-25.JPG | side = left | caption = The cover shows the 1923 U.S. Women's Amateur Champion, Edith Cummings. | title = Time | publisher = [[Time Warner]] | text = '''''Time''''' (trademarked in capitals as '''''TIME''''') is an American [[news magazine]]... | link = Time (magazine) }} </syntaxhighlight>
will produce:
{{Selected publication | image = Time Cover in 1924-08-25.JPG | side = left | caption = The cover shows the 1923 U.S. Women's Amateur Champion, Edith Cummings. | title = Time | publisher = [[Time Warner]] | text = '''''Time''''' (trademarked in capitals as '''''TIME''''') is an American [[news magazine]], similar to ''[[Newsweek]]'' and ''[[U.S. News & World Report]].'' A European edition (''Time Europe'', formerly known as ''Time Atlantic'') is published from London. ''Time Europe'' covers the Middle East, Africa and, since 2003, Latin America. An Asian edition (''Time Asia'') is based in Hong Kong. As of 2009, ''Time'' no longer publishes a Canadian advertiser edition. The South Pacific edition, covering Australia, New Zealand and the Pacific Islands, is based in [[Sydney]]. In some advertising campaigns, the magazine has suggested that, through a [[Backronym|bacronym]], the letters ''T-I-M-E'' stand for '''''The International Magazine of Events'''''. | link = Time (magazine) }}
=== Comic ===
<syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext" width="75%"> {{Selected publication | image = | caption = The movie version of the Batmobile | title = [[Batman]] | comic = yes | creator = [[Bob Kane]], [[Bill Finger]] | author = [[Dennis O'Neil]] | artist = [[FRank Miller (comics)|FRank Miller]] | publisher = [[DC Comics]] | page = comics | text = '''Batman''' (originally referred to as '''the Bat-Man''' and still referred to at times as '''the Batman''') ... | link = Batman }} </syntaxhighlight>
will produce
{{Selected publication | image = | caption = The movie version of the Batmobile | title = [[Batman]] | comic = yes | creator = [[Bob Kane]], [[Bill Finger]] | author = [[Dennis O'Neil]] | artist = [[FRank Miller (comics)|FRank Miller]] | publisher = [[DC Comics]] | page = comics | text = '''Batman''' (originally referred to as '''the Bat-Man''' and still referred to at times as '''the Batman''') is a [[Character (arts)|fictional character]], a [[comic book]] [[superhero]] co-created by artist [[Bob Kane]] and writer [[Bill Finger]] (although only Kane receives official credit), appearing in publications by [[DC Comics]]. The character first appeared in ''[[Detective Comics]]'' #27 in May 1939. Batman's [[secret identity]] is '''Bruce Wayne''', a wealthy industrialist, playboy, and philanthropist. Witnessing the murder of his parents as a child, Wayne trains himself both physically and intellectually and dons a bat-themed costume in order to fight crime. Batman operates in the fictional American [[Gotham City]], assisted by various supporting characters including his sidekick [[Robin (character)|Robin]] and his butler [[Alfred Pennyworth]], and fights an assortment of villains influenced by the characters' roots in film and [[pulp magazine]]s. Unlike most superheroes, he does not possess any [[Superpower (ability)|superpowers]]; he makes use of intellect, detective skills, science and technology, wealth, physical prowess, and intimidation in his war on crime. | link = Batman }}
== See also == {{Portal template list}}
<includeonly>{{Sandbox other|| <!--Categories below here, please; interwikis to Wikidata.--> [[Category:Wikipedia Portal templates|Selected Publication]]
}}</includeonly>

This template is used to format the articles found in the '''''Selected publication''''' sub-page found on some Wikipedia Portal main pages. Use it on the article pages that are linked to the Selected publication sub-page, e.g. '''Portal:''Foo''/Selected publication/1'''. For the quickest and easiest way to create the '''Portal:''Foo''/Selected ''bar''''' sub-page and related article links, use one of the {{tl|Numbered subpages}} templates.
== Template layout == <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext" width="75%"> {{Selected publication | image = | size = | side = | caption = | title = | comic = | author = | artist = | publisher = | page = <!--- Required ---> | text = <!--- Required ---> | link = <!--- Required ---> }} </syntaxhighlight>
== Usage == It is unnecessary to include braces, quotation marks or other wiki-markup in the template. The '''''|text=''''' field does allow the use of wiki-markup at the contributor's discretion.
* '''image''' - Places an image relating to the subject of the article on the left side of the section. * '''size''' - Sets the image size, if left out or left blank it will default to 150px. Values must be entered '''''xxx''px'''. * '''side''' - this field sets the alignment of the image; acceptable values are ''right'' or ''center''. * '''caption''' - Creates a caption for the image. * '''title''' - the title of the work, it will automatically italicize it * '''comic''' - a "yes" in this field will add the option to include an artists name * '''author''' - the creator of the work * '''editor''' - a "yes" in this field will change the ''By'' to ''Edited by'' in the author field. It will not work if the comic switch is set to yes. * '''artist''' - The artist of the work, it will only work if the ''|comic='' field is set to yes * '''publisher'''- the publisher name is listed here * '''page''' - is used to set the page that the product list resides on, e.g. ''|name=comic'' would set the '''More selected ''publications''''' link to look for the sub-pages at '''Portal:Foo/Selected comic''' as well as setting the text of the link to '''More selected ''comics'''''. '''Do not pluralize!''' * '''text''' - A brief summary of the subject. * '''link''' - sets the link in the '''Read more...''' that points to the full article.
== Example ==
=== Generic === <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext"> {{Selected publication | image = File:Time Cover in 1924-08-25.JPG | side = left | caption = The cover shows the 1923 U.S. Women's Amateur Champion, Edith Cummings. | title = Time | publisher = [[Time Warner]] | text = '''''Time''''' (trademarked in capitals as '''''TIME''''') is an American [[news magazine]]... | link = Time (magazine) }} </syntaxhighlight>
will produce:
{{Selected publication | image = Time Cover in 1924-08-25.JPG | side = left | caption = The cover shows the 1923 U.S. Women's Amateur Champion, Edith Cummings. | title = Time | publisher = [[Time Warner]] | text = '''''Time''''' (trademarked in capitals as '''''TIME''''') is an American [[news magazine]], similar to ''[[Newsweek]]'' and ''[[U.S. News & World Report]].'' A European edition (''Time Europe'', formerly known as ''Time Atlantic'') is published from London. ''Time Europe'' covers the Middle East, Africa and, since 2003, Latin America. An Asian edition (''Time Asia'') is based in Hong Kong. As of 2009, ''Time'' no longer publishes a Canadian advertiser edition. The South Pacific edition, covering Australia, New Zealand and the Pacific Islands, is based in [[Sydney]]. In some advertising campaigns, the magazine has suggested that, through a [[:en:Backronym|bacronym]], the letters ''T-I-M-E'' stand for '''''The International Magazine of Events'''''. | link = Time (magazine) }}
=== Comic ===
<syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext" width="75%"> {{Selected publication | image = | caption = The movie version of the Batmobile | title = [[:en:Batman|Batman]] | comic = yes | creator = [[Bob Kane]], [[Bill Finger]] | author = [[Dennis O'Neil]] | artist = [[FRank Miller (comics)|FRank Miller]] | publisher = [[:en:DC Comics|DC Comics]] | page = comics | text = '''Batman''' (originally referred to as '''the Bat-Man''' and still referred to at times as '''the Batman''') ... | link = Batman }} </syntaxhighlight>
will produce
{{Selected publication | image = | caption = The movie version of the Batmobile | title = [[:en:Batman|Batman]] | comic = yes | creator = [[Bob Kane]], [[Bill Finger]] | author = [[Dennis O'Neil]] | artist = [[FRank Miller (comics)|FRank Miller]] | publisher = [[:en:DC Comics|DC Comics]] | page = comics | text = '''Batman''' (originally referred to as '''the Bat-Man''' and still referred to at times as '''the Batman''') is a [[:en:Character (arts)|fictional character]], a [[comic book]] [[superhero]] co-created by artist [[Bob Kane]] and writer [[Bill Finger]] (although only Kane receives official credit), appearing in publications by [[:en:DC Comics|DC Comics]]. The character first appeared in ''[[Detective Comics]]'' #27 in May 1939. Batman's [[secret identity]] is '''Bruce Wayne''', a wealthy industrialist, playboy, and philanthropist. Witnessing the murder of his parents as a child, Wayne trains himself both physically and intellectually and dons a bat-themed costume in order to fight crime. Batman operates in the fictional American [[Gotham City]], assisted by various supporting characters including his sidekick [[Robin (character)|Robin]] and his butler [[:en:Alfred Pennyworth|Alfred Pennyworth]], and fights an assortment of villains influenced by the characters' roots in film and [[pulp magazine]]s. Unlike most superheroes, he does not possess any [[Superpower (ability)|superpowers]]; he makes use of intellect, detective skills, science and technology, wealth, physical prowess, and intimidation in his war on crime. | link = Batman }}
== See also == {{Portal template list}}
<includeonly>{{Sandbox other|| <!--Categories below here, please; interwikis to Wikidata.--> [[Category:Wikipedia Portal templates|Selected Publication]]
}}</includeonly>

Batman operates in the fictional American [[Gotham City]], assisted by various supporting characters including his sidekick [[Robin (character)|Robin]] and his butler [[:en:Alfred Pennyworth|Alfred Pennyworth]], and fights an assortment of villains influenced by the characters' roots in film and [[pulp magazine]]s. Unlike most superheroes, he does not possess any [[Superpower (ability)|superpowers]]; he makes use of intellect, detective skills, science and technology, wealth, physical prowess, and intimidation in his war on crime. | link = Batman }}
== See also == {{Portal template list}}

}}</includeonly>

revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} <!-- PLEASE ADD CATEGORIES AND INTERWIKIS AT THE BOTTOM OF THIS PAGE -->
This template is used to format the articles found in the '''''Selected publication''''' sub-page found on some Wikipedia Portal main pages. Use it on the article pages that are linked to the Selected publication sub-page, e.g. '''Portal:''Foo''/Selected publication/1'''. For the quickest and easiest way to create the '''Portal:''Foo''/Selected ''bar''''' sub-page and related article links, use one of the {{tl|Numbered subpages}} templates.
== Template layout == <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext" width="75%"> {{Selected publication | image = | size = | side = | caption = | title = | comic = | author = | artist = | publisher = | page = <!--- Required ---> | text = <!--- Required ---> | link = <!--- Required ---> }} </syntaxhighlight>
== Usage == It is unnecessary to include braces, quotation marks or other wiki-markup in the template. The '''''|text=''''' field does allow the use of wiki-markup at the contributor's discretion.
* '''image''' - Places an image relating to the subject of the article on the left side of the section. * '''size''' - Sets the image size, if left out or left blank it will default to 150px. Values must be entered '''''xxx''px'''. * '''side''' - this field sets the alignment of the image; acceptable values are ''right'' or ''center''. * '''caption''' - Creates a caption for the image. * '''title''' - the title of the work, it will automatically italicize it * '''comic''' - a "yes" in this field will add the option to include an artists name * '''author''' - the creator of the work * '''editor''' - a "yes" in this field will change the ''By'' to ''Edited by'' in the author field. It will not work if the comic switch is set to yes. * '''artist''' - The artist of the work, it will only work if the ''|comic='' field is set to yes * '''publisher'''- the publisher name is listed here * '''page''' - is used to set the page that the product list resides on, e.g. ''|name=comic'' would set the '''More selected ''publications''''' link to look for the sub-pages at '''Portal:Foo/Selected comic''' as well as setting the text of the link to '''More selected ''comics'''''. '''Do not pluralize!''' * '''text''' - A brief summary of the subject. * '''link''' - sets the link in the '''Read more...''' that points to the full article.
== Example ==
=== Generic === <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext"> {{Selected publication | image = File:Time Cover in 1924-08-25.JPG | side = left | caption = The cover shows the 1923 U.S. Women's Amateur Champion, Edith Cummings. | title = Time | publisher = [[Time Warner]] | text = '''''Time''''' (trademarked in capitals as '''''TIME''''') is an American [[news magazine]]... | link = Time (magazine) }} </syntaxhighlight>
will produce:
{{Selected publication | image = Time Cover in 1924-08-25.JPG | side = left | caption = The cover shows the 1923 U.S. Women's Amateur Champion, Edith Cummings. | title = Time | publisher = [[Time Warner]] | text = '''''Time''''' (trademarked in capitals as '''''TIME''''') is an American [[news magazine]], similar to ''[[Newsweek]]'' and ''[[U.S. News & World Report]].'' A European edition (''Time Europe'', formerly known as ''Time Atlantic'') is published from London. ''Time Europe'' covers the Middle East, Africa and, since 2003, Latin America. An Asian edition (''Time Asia'') is based in Hong Kong. As of 2009, ''Time'' no longer publishes a Canadian advertiser edition. The South Pacific edition, covering Australia, New Zealand and the Pacific Islands, is based in [[Sydney]]. In some advertising campaigns, the magazine has suggested that, through a [[:en:Backronym|bacronym]], the letters ''T-I-M-E'' stand for '''''The International Magazine of Events'''''. | link = Time (magazine) }}
=== Comic ===
<syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext" width="75%"> {{Selected publication | image = | caption = The movie version of the Batmobile | title = [[:en:Batman|Batman]] | comic = yes | creator = [[:en:Bob Kane|Bob Kane]], [[:en:Bill Finger|Bill Finger]] | author = [[:en:Dennis O'Neil|Dennis O'Neil]] | artist = [[FRank Miller (comics)|FRank Miller]] | publisher = [[:en:DC Comics|DC Comics]] | page = comics | text = '''Batman''' (originally referred to as '''the Bat-Man''' and still referred to at times as '''the Batman''') ... | link = Batman }} </syntaxhighlight>
will produce
{{Selected publication | image = | caption = The movie version of the Batmobile | title = [[:en:Batman|Batman]] | comic = yes | creator = [[:en:Bob Kane|Bob Kane]], [[:en:Bill Finger|Bill Finger]] | author = [[:en:Dennis O'Neil|Dennis O'Neil]] | artist = [[FRank Miller (comics)|FRank Miller]] | publisher = [[:en:DC Comics|DC Comics]] | page = comics | text = '''Batman''' (originally referred to as '''the Bat-Man''' and still referred to at times as '''the Batman''') is a [[:en:Character (arts)|fictional character]], a [[:en:Comic book|Comic book]] [[superhero]] co-created by artist [[:en:Bob Kane|Bob Kane]] and writer [[:en:Bill Finger|Bill Finger]] (although only Kane receives official credit), appearing in publications by [[:en:DC Comics|DC Comics]]. The character first appeared in ''[[:en:Detective Comics|Detective Comics]]'' #27 in May 1939. Batman's [[secret identity]] is '''Bruce Wayne''', a wealthy industrialist, playboy, and philanthropist. Witnessing the murder of his parents as a child, Wayne trains himself both physically and intellectually and dons a bat-themed costume in order to fight crime. Batman operates in the fictional American [[Gotham City]], assisted by various supporting characters including his sidekick [[Robin (character)|Robin]] and his butler [[:en:Alfred Pennyworth|Alfred Pennyworth]], and fights an assortment of villains influenced by the characters' roots in film and [[pulp magazine]]s. Unlike most superheroes, he does not possess any [[Superpower (ability)|superpowers]]; he makes use of intellect, detective skills, science and technology, wealth, physical prowess, and intimidation in his war on crime. | link = Batman }}
== See also == {{Portal template list}}
<includeonly>{{Sandbox other|| <!--Categories below here, please; interwikis to Wikidata.--> [[Category:Wikipedia Portal templates|Selected Publication]]
}}</includeonly>

The South Pacific edition, covering Australia, New Zealand and the Pacific Islands, is based in [[Sydney]]. In some advertising campaigns, the magazine has suggested that, through a [[:en:Backronym|bacronym]], the letters ''T-I-M-E'' stand for '''''The International Magazine of Events'''''. | link = Time (magazine) }}
=== Comic ===
<syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext" width="75%"> {{Selected publication | image = | caption = The movie version of the Batmobile | title = [[:en:Batman|Batman]] | comic = yes | creator = [[:en:Bob Kane|Bob Kane]], [[:en:Bill Finger|Bill Finger]] | author = [[:en:Dennis O'Neil|Dennis O'Neil]] | artist = [[FRank Miller (comics)|FRank Miller]] | publisher = [[:en:DC Comics|DC Comics]] | page = comics | text = '''Batman''' (originally referred to as '''the Bat-Man''' and still referred to at times as '''the Batman''') ... | link = Batman }} </syntaxhighlight>
will produce
{{Selected publication | image = | caption = The movie version of the Batmobile | title = [[:en:Batman|Batman]] | comic = yes | creator = [[:en:Bob Kane|Bob Kane]], [[:en:Bill Finger|Bill Finger]] | author = [[:en:Dennis O'Neil|Dennis O'Neil]] | artist = [[FRank Miller (comics)|FRank Miller]] | publisher = [[:en:DC Comics|DC Comics]] | page = comics | text = '''Batman''' (originally referred to as '''the Bat-Man''' and still referred to at times as '''the Batman''') is a [[:en:Character (arts)|fictional character]], a [[:en:Comic book|Comic book]] [[superhero]] co-created by artist [[:en:Bob Kane|Bob Kane]] and writer [[:en:Bill Finger|Bill Finger]] (although only Kane receives official credit), appearing in publications by [[:en:DC Comics|DC Comics]]. The character first appeared in ''[[:en:Detective Comics|Detective Comics]]'' #27 in May 1939. Batman's [[secret identity]] is '''Bruce Wayne''', a wealthy industrialist, playboy, and philanthropist. Witnessing the murder of his parents as a child, Wayne trains himself both physically and intellectually and dons a bat-themed costume in order to fight crime. Batman operates in the fictional American [[:en:Gotham City|Gotham City]], assisted by various supporting characters including his sidekick [[Robin (character)|Robin]] and his butler [[:en:Alfred Pennyworth|Alfred Pennyworth]], and fights an assortment of villains influenced by the characters' roots in film and [[:en:pulp magazine|pulp magazine]]s. Unlike most superheroes, he does not possess any [[Superpower (ability)|superpowers]]; he makes use of intellect, detective skills, science and technology, wealth, physical prowess, and intimidation in his war on crime. | link = Batman }}
== See also == {{Portal template list}}

}}</includeonly>

{{Documentation subpage}} <!-- PLEASE ADD CATEGORIES AND INTERWIKIS AT THE BOTTOM OF THIS PAGE -->
This template is used to format the articles found in the '''''Selected publication''''' sub-page found on some Wikipedia Portal main pages. Use it on the article pages that are linked to the Selected publication sub-page, e.g. '''Portal:''Foo''/Selected publication/1'''. For the quickest and easiest way to create the '''Portal:''Foo''/Selected ''bar''''' sub-page and related article links, use one of the {{tl|Numbered subpages}} templates.
== Template layout == <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext" width="75%"> {{Selected publication | image = | size = | side = | caption = | title = | comic = | author = | artist = | publisher = | page = <!--- Required ---> | text = <!--- Required ---> | link = <!--- Required ---> }} </syntaxhighlight>
== Usage == It is unnecessary to include braces, quotation marks or other wiki-markup in the template. The '''''|text=''''' field does allow the use of wiki-markup at the contributor's discretion.
* '''image''' - Places an image relating to the subject of the article on the left side of the section. * '''size''' - Sets the image size, if left out or left blank it will default to 150px. Values must be entered '''''xxx''px'''. * '''side''' - this field sets the alignment of the image; acceptable values are ''right'' or ''center''. * '''caption''' - Creates a caption for the image. * '''title''' - the title of the work, it will automatically italicize it * '''comic''' - a "yes" in this field will add the option to include an artists name * '''author''' - the creator of the work * '''editor''' - a "yes" in this field will change the ''By'' to ''Edited by'' in the author field. It will not work if the comic switch is set to yes. * '''artist''' - The artist of the work, it will only work if the ''|comic='' field is set to yes * '''publisher'''- the publisher name is listed here * '''page''' - is used to set the page that the product list resides on, e.g. ''|name=comic'' would set the '''More selected ''publications''''' link to look for the sub-pages at '''Portal:Foo/Selected comic''' as well as setting the text of the link to '''More selected ''comics'''''. '''Do not pluralize!''' * '''text''' - A brief summary of the subject. * '''link''' - sets the link in the '''Read more...''' that points to the full article.
== Example ==
=== Generic === <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext"> {{Selected publication | image = File:Time Cover in 1924-08-25.JPG | side = left | caption = The cover shows the 1923 U.S. Women's Amateur Champion, Edith Cummings. | title = Time | publisher = [[Time Warner]] | text = '''''Time''''' (trademarked in capitals as '''''TIME''''') is an American [[:en:news magazine|news magazine]]... | link = Time (magazine) }} </syntaxhighlight>

== Usage == It is unnecessary to include braces, quotation marks or other wiki-markup in the template. The '''''|text=''''' field does allow the use of wiki-markup at the contributor's discretion.
* '''image''' - Places an image relating to the subject of the article on the left side of the section. * '''size''' - Sets the image size, if left out or left blank it will default to 150px. Values must be entered '''''xxx''px'''. * '''side''' - this field sets the alignment of the image; acceptable values are ''right'' or ''center''. * '''caption''' - Creates a caption for the image. * '''title''' - the title of the work, it will automatically italicize it * '''comic''' - a "yes" in this field will add the option to include an artists name * '''author''' - the creator of the work * '''editor''' - a "yes" in this field will change the ''By'' to ''Edited by'' in the author field. It will not work if the comic switch is set to yes. * '''artist''' - The artist of the work, it will only work if the ''|comic='' field is set to yes * '''publisher'''- the publisher name is listed here * '''page''' - is used to set the page that the product list resides on, e.g. ''|name=comic'' would set the '''More selected ''publications''''' link to look for the sub-pages at '''Portal:Foo/Selected comic''' as well as setting the text of the link to '''More selected ''comics'''''. '''Do not pluralize!''' * '''text''' - A brief summary of the subject. * '''link''' - sets the link in the '''Read more...''' that points to the full article.
== Example ==
=== Generic === <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext"> {{Selected publication | image = File:Time Cover in 1924-08-25.JPG | side = left | caption = The cover shows the 1923 U.S. Women's Amateur Champion, Edith Cummings. | title = Time | publisher = [[Time Warner]] | text = '''''Time''''' (trademarked in capitals as '''''TIME''''') is an American [[:en:news magazine|news magazine]]... | link = Time (magazine) }} </syntaxhighlight>
will produce:
{{Selected publication | image = Time Cover in 1924-08-25.JPG | side = left | caption = The cover shows the 1923 U.S. Women's Amateur Champion, Edith Cummings. | title = Time | publisher = [[Time Warner]] | text = '''''Time''''' (trademarked in capitals as '''''TIME''''') is an American [[:en:news magazine|news magazine]], similar to ''[[:en:Newsweek|Newsweek]]'' and ''[[:en:U.S. News & World Report|U.S. News & World Report]].'' A European edition (''Time Europe'', formerly known as ''Time Atlantic'') is published from London. ''Time Europe'' covers the Middle East, Africa and, since 2003, Latin America. An Asian edition (''Time Asia'') is based in Hong Kong. As of 2009, ''Time'' no longer publishes a Canadian advertiser edition. The South Pacific edition, covering Australia, New Zealand and the Pacific Islands, is based in [[Sydney]]. In some advertising campaigns, the magazine has suggested that, through a [[:en:Backronym|bacronym]], the letters ''T-I-M-E'' stand for '''''The International Magazine of Events'''''. | link = Time (magazine) }}
=== Comic ===
<syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext" width="75%"> {{Selected publication | image = | caption = The movie version of the Batmobile | title = [[:en:Batman|Batman]] | comic = yes | creator = [[:en:Bob Kane|Bob Kane]], [[:en:Bill Finger|Bill Finger]] | author = [[:en:Dennis O'Neil|Dennis O'Neil]] | artist = [[FRank Miller (comics)|FRank Miller]] | publisher = [[:en:DC Comics|DC Comics]] | page = comics | text = '''Batman''' (originally referred to as '''the Bat-Man''' and still referred to at times as '''the Batman''') ... | link = Batman }} </syntaxhighlight>
will produce
{{Selected publication | image = | caption = The movie version of the Batmobile | title = [[:en:Batman|Batman]] | comic = yes | creator = [[:en:Bob Kane|Bob Kane]], [[:en:Bill Finger|Bill Finger]] | author = [[:en:Dennis O'Neil|Dennis O'Neil]] | artist = [[FRank Miller (comics)|FRank Miller]] | publisher = [[:en:DC Comics|DC Comics]] | page = comics | text = '''Batman''' (originally referred to as '''the Bat-Man''' and still referred to at times as '''the Batman''') is a [[:en:Character (arts)|fictional character]], a [[:en:Comic book|Comic book]] [[:en:Superhero|Superhero]] co-created by artist [[:en:Bob Kane|Bob Kane]] and writer [[:en:Bill Finger|Bill Finger]] (although only Kane receives official credit), appearing in publications by [[:en:DC Comics|DC Comics]]. The character first appeared in ''[[:en:Detective Comics|Detective Comics]]'' #27 in May 1939. Batman's [[:en:secret identity|secret identity]] is '''Bruce Wayne''', a wealthy industrialist, playboy, and philanthropist. Witnessing the murder of his parents as a child, Wayne trains himself both physically and intellectually and dons a bat-themed costume in order to fight crime. Batman operates in the fictional American [[:en:Gotham City|Gotham City]], assisted by various supporting characters including his sidekick [[:en:Robin (character)|Robin]] and his butler [[:en:Alfred Pennyworth|Alfred Pennyworth]], and fights an assortment of villains influenced by the characters' roots in film and [[:en:pulp magazine|pulp magazine]]s. Unlike most superheroes, he does not possess any [[:en:Superpower (ability)|superpowers]]; he makes use of intellect, detective skills, science and technology, wealth, physical prowess, and intimidation in his war on crime. | link = Batman }}
== See also == {{Portal template list}}
<includeonly>{{Sandbox other|| <!--Categories below here, please; interwikis to Wikidata.--> [[Category:Wikipedia Portal templates|Selected Publication]]
}}</includeonly>

In some advertising campaigns, the magazine has suggested that, through a [[:en:Backronym|bacronym]], the letters ''T-I-M-E'' stand for '''''The International Magazine of Events'''''. | link = Time (magazine) }}
=== Comic ===
<syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext" width="75%"> {{Selected publication | image = | caption = The movie version of the Batmobile | title = [[:en:Batman|Batman]] | comic = yes | creator = [[:en:Bob Kane|Bob Kane]], [[:en:Bill Finger|Bill Finger]] | author = [[:en:Dennis O'Neil|Dennis O'Neil]] | artist = [[FRank Miller (comics)|FRank Miller]] | publisher = [[:en:DC Comics|DC Comics]] | page = comics | text = '''Batman''' (originally referred to as '''the Bat-Man''' and still referred to at times as '''the Batman''') ... | link = Batman }} </syntaxhighlight>
will produce
{{Selected publication | image = | caption = The movie version of the Batmobile | title = [[:en:Batman|Batman]] | comic = yes | creator = [[:en:Bob Kane|Bob Kane]], [[:en:Bill Finger|Bill Finger]] | author = [[:en:Dennis O'Neil|Dennis O'Neil]] | artist = [[FRank Miller (comics)|FRank Miller]] | publisher = [[:en:DC Comics|DC Comics]] | page = comics | text = '''Batman''' (originally referred to as '''the Bat-Man''' and still referred to at times as '''the Batman''') is a [[:en:Character (arts)|fictional character]], a [[:en:Comic book|Comic book]] [[:en:Superhero|Superhero]] co-created by artist [[:en:Bob Kane|Bob Kane]] and writer [[:en:Bill Finger|Bill Finger]] (although only Kane receives official credit), appearing in publications by [[:en:DC Comics|DC Comics]]. The character first appeared in ''[[:en:Detective Comics|Detective Comics]]'' #27 in May 1939. Batman's [[:en:secret identity|secret identity]] is '''Bruce Wayne''', a wealthy industrialist, playboy, and philanthropist. Witnessing the murder of his parents as a child, Wayne trains himself both physically and intellectually and dons a bat-themed costume in order to fight crime. Batman operates in the fictional American [[:en:Gotham City|Gotham City]], assisted by various supporting characters including his sidekick [[:en:Robin (character)|Robin]] and his butler [[:en:Alfred Pennyworth|Alfred Pennyworth]], and fights an assortment of villains influenced by the characters' roots in film and [[:en:pulp magazine|pulp magazine]]s. Unlike most superheroes, he does not possess any [[:en:Superpower (ability)|superpowers]]; he makes use of intellect, detective skills, science and technology, wealth, physical prowess, and intimidation in his war on crime. | link = Batman }}
== See also == {{Portal template list}}

}}</includeonly>

{{Documentation subpage}} <!-- PLEASE ADD CATEGORIES AND INTERWIKIS AT THE BOTTOM OF THIS PAGE -->
This template is used to format the articles found in the '''''Selected publication''''' sub-page found on some Wikipedia Portal main pages. Use it on the article pages that are linked to the Selected publication sub-page, e.g. '''Portal:''Foo''/Selected publication/1'''. For the quickest and easiest way to create the '''Portal:''Foo''/Selected ''bar''''' sub-page and related article links, use one of the {{tl|Numbered subpages}} templates.
== Template layout == <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext" width="75%"> {{Selected publication | image = | size = | side = | caption = | title = | comic = | author = | artist = | publisher = | page = <!--- Required ---> | text = <!--- Required ---> | link = <!--- Required ---> }} </syntaxhighlight>
== Usage == It is unnecessary to include braces, quotation marks or other wiki-markup in the template. The '''''|text=''''' field does allow the use of wiki-markup at the contributor's discretion.
* '''image''' - Places an image relating to the subject of the article on the left side of the section. * '''size''' - Sets the image size, if left out or left blank it will default to 150px. Values must be entered '''''xxx''px'''. * '''side''' - this field sets the alignment of the image; acceptable values are ''right'' or ''center''. * '''caption''' - Creates a caption for the image. * '''title''' - the title of the work, it will automatically italicize it * '''comic''' - a "yes" in this field will add the option to include an artists name * '''author''' - the creator of the work * '''editor''' - a "yes" in this field will change the ''By'' to ''Edited by'' in the author field. It will not work if the comic switch is set to yes. * '''artist''' - The artist of the work, it will only work if the ''|comic='' field is set to yes * '''publisher'''- the publisher name is listed here * '''page''' - is used to set the page that the product list resides on, e.g. ''|name=comic'' would set the '''More selected ''publications''''' link to look for the sub-pages at '''Portal:Foo/Selected comic''' as well as setting the text of the link to '''More selected ''comics'''''. '''Do not pluralize!''' * '''text''' - A brief summary of the subject. * '''link''' - sets the link in the '''Read more...''' that points to the full article.
== Example ==
=== Generic === <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext"> {{Selected publication | image = File:Time Cover in 1924-08-25.JPG | side = left | caption = The cover shows the 1923 U.S. Women's Amateur Champion, Edith Cummings. | title = Time | publisher = [[:en:Time Warner|Time Warner]] | text = '''''Time''''' (trademarked in capitals as '''''TIME''''') is an American [[:en:news magazine|news magazine]]... | link = Time (magazine) }} </syntaxhighlight>
will produce:
{{Selected publication | image = Time Cover in 1924-08-25.JPG | side = left | caption = The cover shows the 1923 U.S. Women's Amateur Champion, Edith Cummings. | title = Time | publisher = [[:en:Time Warner|Time Warner]] | text = '''''Time''''' (trademarked in capitals as '''''TIME''''') is an American [[:en:news magazine|news magazine]], similar to ''[[:en:Newsweek|Newsweek]]'' and ''[[:en:U.S. News & World Report|U.S. News & World Report]].'' A European edition (''Time Europe'', formerly known as ''Time Atlantic'') is published from London. ''Time Europe'' covers the Middle East, Africa and, since 2003, Latin America. An Asian edition (''Time Asia'') is based in Hong Kong. As of 2009, ''Time'' no longer publishes a Canadian advertiser edition. The South Pacific edition, covering Australia, New Zealand and the Pacific Islands, is based in [[:en:Sydney|Sydney]]. In some advertising campaigns, the magazine has suggested that, through a [[:en:Backronym|bacronym]], the letters ''T-I-M-E'' stand for '''''The International Magazine of Events'''''. | link = Time (magazine) }}
=== Comic ===
<syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext" width="75%"> {{Selected publication | image = | caption = The movie version of the Batmobile | title = [[:en:Batman|Batman]] | comic = yes | creator = [[:en:Bob Kane|Bob Kane]], [[:en:Bill Finger|Bill Finger]] | author = [[:en:Dennis O'Neil|Dennis O'Neil]] | artist = [[FRank Miller (comics)|FRank Miller]] | publisher = [[:en:DC Comics|DC Comics]] | page = comics | text = '''Batman''' (originally referred to as '''the Bat-Man''' and still referred to at times as '''the Batman''') ... | link = Batman }} </syntaxhighlight>
will produce
{{Selected publication | image = | caption = The movie version of the Batmobile | title = [[:en:Batman|Batman]] | comic = yes | creator = [[:en:Bob Kane|Bob Kane]], [[:en:Bill Finger|Bill Finger]] | author = [[:en:Dennis O'Neil|Dennis O'Neil]] | artist = [[FRank Miller (comics)|FRank Miller]] | publisher = [[:en:DC Comics|DC Comics]] | page = comics | text = '''Batman''' (originally referred to as '''the Bat-Man''' and still referred to at times as '''the Batman''') is a [[:en:Character (arts)|fictional character]], a [[:en:Comic book|Comic book]] [[:en:Superhero|Superhero]] co-created by artist [[:en:Bob Kane|Bob Kane]] and writer [[:en:Bill Finger|Bill Finger]] (although only Kane receives official credit), appearing in publications by [[:en:DC Comics|DC Comics]]. The character first appeared in ''[[:en:Detective Comics|Detective Comics]]'' #27 in May 1939. Batman's [[:en:secret identity|secret identity]] is '''Bruce Wayne''', a wealthy industrialist, playboy, and philanthropist. Witnessing the murder of his parents as a child, Wayne trains himself both physically and intellectually and dons a bat-themed costume in order to fight crime. Batman operates in the fictional American [[:en:Gotham City|Gotham City]], assisted by various supporting characters including his sidekick [[:en:Robin (character)|Robin]] and his butler [[:en:Alfred Pennyworth|Alfred Pennyworth]], and fights an assortment of villains influenced by the characters' roots in film and [[:en:pulp magazine|pulp magazine]]s. Unlike most superheroes, he does not possess any [[:en:Superpower (ability)|superpowers]]; he makes use of intellect, detective skills, science and technology, wealth, physical prowess, and intimidation in his war on crime. | link = Batman }}
== See also == {{Portal template list}}

}}</includeonly> [[Category:Templates]]
